obsidian.$description: Obsidian — Page canvas, deepest section backgrounds, hairline borders on text

charcoal.$description: Charcoal — Elevated section surfaces, card backgrounds, input fields — the middle layer between pure black and white

paper-white.$description: Paper White — Primary display and body text on dark surfaces, default borders, icon strokes, form controls

ash.$description: Ash — Hairline borders, dividers, input outlines, and card edges on light surfaces.

feature-deck.$description: Display and heading type — used exclusively for the largest editorial moments: hero headline, section openers, and the service index. The 300 weight (light/thin) with tight negative tracking is the signature move: most display serifs default to 400–700, so the whisper-weight conveys authority through restraint rather than volume. Letter-spacing tightens from -0.009em at 82px to -0.018em at 116px.

shne.$description: All functional and body type — navigation, links, body copy, list items, input fields, buttons, footer. Weight 300 for secondary labels and 400 for body. A neo-grotesque with humanist warmth that stays invisible so the serif can speak.

obsidian-canvas.$description: Surface level 1: Base page background, hero sections, footer

charcoal-surface.$description: Surface level 2: Alternate section bands, content blocks, elevated cards, input fields

hairline-layer.$description: Surface level 3: Structural borders and dividers separating content zones
